keyPoints: E-Waste will be regulated under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
The 'E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2011' has been amended and updated to make it more stringent.
The 2023 amendment introduced the concept of 'Design for Recyclability' and 'Design for Disassembly'.
The 2024 amendment introduced the concept of 'Extended Producer Responsibility' (EPR) for e-waste management.
The E-Waste Management Rules, 2011 mandates e-waste producers to register with the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) and follow prescribed guidelines.
The 'Design for Recyclability' and 'Design for Disassembly' concepts aim to reduce e-waste generation by designing products with recyclability and disassemblability in mind.
EPR requires e-waste producers to take responsibility for the collection, processing, and disposal of their products at the end of their life cycle.
The amendment rules also introduced a new waste category, 'Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)', to manage hazardous e-waste.
The E-Waste (Management) Amendment Rules, 2023, 2024, aim to reduce e-waste generation, promote sustainable consumption, and protect the environment.
